H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, confidence=strong) achieves the highest overstimulation efficacy (66% positive). Qualitative feedback emphasizes rapid improvement and strong music love. YouTube views (30) exceed most others but lack retention metrics. AutoGluon under‑predicts (6.86), suggesting the model may undervalue music affinity. Distribution is right‑skewed with limited bimodality.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: This is a favorite for overstimulation, with many loving the music and seeing improvement in ten minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, confidence=strong) maintains solid overstimulation outcomes (65% positive). Listeners report balanced rhythms but mixed music opinions. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, slightly above observed. Distribution is fairly symmetric.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: It helps many manage overstimulation, though some are neutral on the music.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) continues strong overstimulation performance (64% positive). Qualitative notes indicate quick settlement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views are modest (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a modest uplift. Distribution shows mild right skew.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners report quick calming benefits for overstimulation, with steady rhythm feedback.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, confidence=strong) shows robust anxiety relief with 63% positive ratings. Qualitative feedback notes moderate to significant improvement within ~10 minutes and a predominantly balanced rhythm. YouTube reach is minimal (7 views), indicating limited discovery. AutoGluon predicts 7.84, modestly above the observed mean, suggesting room for incremental gains. No bimodality detected; response distribution leans toward higher scores.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: People generally find this calming for anxiety, noticing improvement within about ten minutes.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) yields consistent anxiety outcomes (67% positive). Listeners report noticeable improvement, often within 5 minutes, and describe the rhythm as balanced yet music opinion splits (neutral to liked). YouTube views are low (6). AutoGluon forecasts 7.54, a ~0.6‑point uplift, hinting at untapped potential. Distribution remains unimodal.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Most listeners report a noticeable reduction in anxiety, though some feel the music is neutral.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, confidence=meaningful) maintains strong anxiety scores (61% positive). Qualitative notes highlight very settling rhythms and improvement typically after 10 minutes. Music opinion is mixed, with a roughly even split between liked and neutral. YouTube impressions are scarce (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, modestly higher than observed. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed but not overtly bimodal.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: The protocol often helps with anxiety, especially after ten minutes, though a few find the music less moving.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) reflects expected lower crisis scores (53% positive). Qualitative data includes several reports of significant improvement within 5 minutes, yet 4.6% report no effect. YouTube views are very low (6). AutoGluon predicts 7.96, a substantial delta (>1.5), suggesting delayed entrainment effects not captured by the 1‑second survey. Distribution is fairly symmetric.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: In crisis moments some users feel a strong lift quickly, but overall scores are modest.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, confidence=meaningful) shows modest crisis efficacy (55% positive). Listeners note rapid settlement (≈5 minutes) and mixed music sentiment. YouTube views remain minimal (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.96, a large upward gap, reinforcing the hypothesis of latent benefit. Distribution appears slightly right‑skewed.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Many notice a quick calming effect during acute stress, though music reactions vary.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, confidence=meaningful) yields comparable crisis outcomes (52% positive). Qualitative feedback reports immediate or early settlement for many, with balanced rhythm perception. YouTube visibility is low (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, indicating potential under‑reporting. Distribution shows modest bimodality, with a small tail of low scores.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Listeners often feel immediate relief in crisis, with balanced rhythm perception.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, confidence=strong) leads sleep protocols with high positive ratings (65%). Listeners settle quickly and love the music. YouTube views are low (8). AutoGluon aligns closely (7.54). Distribution is right‑skewed.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: People sleep better after this protocol, often noticing the benefit within a few minutes.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, confidence=meaningful) records solid sleep outcomes (67% positive) but shows some bimodality in music opinion. YouTube views remain minimal (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a slight increase. Distribution is near‑normal with a slight left tail.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Sleep improvement is common, though a few find the rhythm a bit busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, confidence=meaningful) sustains sleep efficacy (64% positive). Qualitative data show varied settlement times and balanced rhythm. YouTube reach is very limited (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly higher. Distribution is symmetric.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Many find it helpful for sleep, with mixed opinions on the music.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, confidence=meaningful) shows lower focus outcomes (56% positive). Qualitative data reveal mixed improvement levels and a rhythm described as slightly busy, potentially distracting. YouTube views are minimal (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.73, a substantial upward gap, indicating the model expects higher efficacy than surveyed. Distribution shows a modest left tail.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Focus improves for many, though some find the rhythm slightly busy.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, confidence=meaningful) records strong focus improvement (66% positive). Listeners note moderate to significant gains within 10 minutes, with balanced rhythm perception. YouTube reach remains low (4). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, aligning closely. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners report strong focus gains and balanced rhythm, with mixed music feelings.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, confidence=meaningful) attains the highest focus score (71% positive). Qualitative feedback emphasizes significant improvement and a majority settling by 10 minutes; music love rates are high. YouTube views are the highest in THRIVING (15) yet retention data are absent, suggesting discovery gaps. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, matching observed performance. Distribution is broad with a long right tail.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: One of the best focus protocols—most users feel a clear mental boost.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, confidence=meaningful) yields modest creative‑flow scores (53% positive). Listeners report slight improvement and mixed settlement times; rhythm is described as balanced. YouTube views are negligible (2). AutoGluon predicts 7.73, a notable uplift, suggesting the hold‑phase may need extension. Distribution is left‑skewed with a tail toward lower scores.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Creative flow shows modest gains, and listeners settle at different speeds.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, confidence=meaningful) improves modestly (58% positive). Qualitative notes include immediate settlement for some and high rhythm balance. YouTube reach is tiny (2). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly higher. Distribution is relatively tight around the mean.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Some creative users notice improvement after about ten minutes, and like the rhythm.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, confidence=developing) achieves the highest creative‑flow rating (67% positive). Listeners report noticeable to significant improvement, typically within 5–10 minutes, and rhythm is well‑received. YouTube views are low (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, close to observed. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed but not bimodal.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol often boosts creative focus, with many seeing benefits within ten minutes.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, confidence=meaningful) shows solid maintenance outcomes (64% positive). Listeners report moderate improvement, though many settle only after 20 minutes, indicating a potential need for pacing adjustment. YouTube visibility is very low (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, a moderate uplift, and the protocol exhibits extreme CROSS‑RUN DRIFT (similarity = 0.565). Distribution is tight with slight right skew.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Maintenance benefits are decent, but some need a longer settle period.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, confidence=meaningful) displays variable maintenance efficacy (59% positive). Qualitative responses cite mixed rhythm balance and a substantial proportion of neutral music opinion. YouTube views are limited (4). AutoGluon forecasts 7.54, exceeding observed scores. Distribution is wide, reflecting high variance.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed responses on maintenance; rhythm feels okay for many.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, confidence=developing) yields promising maintenance results (71% positive) despite a small sample. Settlements occur quickly for many, yet 4 respondents did not settle, highlighting inconsistency. YouTube exposure is minimal (3). AutoGluon predicts 7.69, a notable uplift, and the protocol registers the lowest similarity (0.557), flagging analytical instability. Distribution shows modest right skew.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Promising maintenance effects, though a few listeners never felt it settle.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, confidence=meaningful) delivers moderate mood/energy outcomes (54% positive). Listeners report varied settlement times and a split between love and neutral music opinion. YouTube views are low (8). AutoGluon predicts 8.23, a large positive delta, indicating the model expects stronger mood impact. Distribution is mildly right‑skewed.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Mood often improves, though the model expects even stronger effects.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, confidence=meaningful) shows slightly better mood scores (64% positive). Qualitative data reveal significant improvement for many and balanced rhythm perception. YouTube views are modest (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed. Distribution is near‑normal.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Listeners report solid mood benefits and balanced rhythm.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) records the lowest mood efficacy (56% positive) with a bimodal pattern (noticeable vs no effect). Listeners settle quickly but music sentiment is mixed. YouTube reach is very low (4). AutoGluon forecasts 7.54, a sizable upward gap, reinforcing the need for rhythm or dosing adjustments. Distribution reflects subgroup divergence.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Mood outcomes are mixed; some feel better quickly, others notice no change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, confidence=meaningful) shows moderate motivation outcomes (45% positive). Listeners note quick settlement (≈5 minutes) but music sentiment is split. YouTube views are low (5). AutoGluon predicts 7.88, a substantial upward gap, suggesting the model expects higher efficacy. Distribution is wide, with a noticeable left tail.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Motivation improves for many, but reactions to the music vary.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X‑MD‑L2 (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, confidence=developing) yields lower motivation scores (43% positive). Qualitative feedback indicates balanced rhythms but mixed music preference. YouTube exposure is modest (7). AutoGluon forecasts 7.54, a sizable increase over observed, highlighting potential underperformance. Distribution is relatively tight.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Motivation gains are modest, and rhythm feels balanced to most listeners.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, confidence=developing) achieves the highest motivation rating in TRANSFORMING (62% positive). Listeners report moderate improvement with quick settlement for many, though variance remains high. YouTube views are modest (7). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, aligning closely. Distribution is broad with a right‑hand tail.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Motivation sees a noticeable lift for several users, though experiences differ.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, confidence=meaningful) provides moderate mind‑clarity benefits (54% positive). Listeners report noticeable improvement within ~5 minutes and balanced rhythms, though music sentiment is split. YouTube views are low (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a moderate increase, suggesting untapped potential. Distribution is fairly symmetric.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Mind clarity improves moderately, with balanced rhythm feedback.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, confidence=developing) improves mind clarity slightly (56% positive). Qualitative data highlight very settling rhythms and a higher proportion of significant improvement. YouTube reach is the strongest in this sub‑group (12 views). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed. Distribution is slightly right‑skewed.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Clarity gains are a bit stronger, and many love the calm rhythm.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, confidence=developing) records the lowest clarity score (50% positive) with a pronounced bimodal distribution, indicating a possible subgroup effect. Qualitative notes mention mixed rhythm opinions (some “too busy”). YouTube views are modest (8) but outcomes remain low. AutoGluon predicts 7.54, a large divergence, underscoring a probable mismatch between protocol design and listener state. The high variance and bimodality flag a strong need for redesign or targeted routing.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: This protocol shows mixed results; some feel it helps, others notice little effect.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The aggregate data suggests modest efficacy across series with distinct variance patterns.
• HEALING shows the highest mean (6.98) and steadier responses across entry points.
• THRIVING attains strong focus outcomes but displays broader variability.
• TRANSFORMING records the lowest mean (6.59) and the widest spread, consistent with its ascending‑arc design.
• Outcome distributions largely align with design intent, though a few protocols diverge.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns reflect their therapeutic arcs.
• HEALING protocols deliver consistent, above‑neutral scores; DESCENT designs show predictable settlement times.
• THRIVING HOLD protocols excel in Focus & Clarity, yet Creative Flow scores lag, indicating possible hold‑phase tuning needs.
• TRANSFORMING ASCENT protocols exhibit higher variance and lower averages, matching expectations for low‑affect states.
• Variance magnitude follows the intended difficulty gradient from HEALING to TRANSFORMING.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Certain protocols exhibit signals that merit focused investigation.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7) shows a bimodal distribution and low efficacy, suggesting a population mismatch.
• T-MB-L3 displays extreme CROSS‑RUN DRIFT (similarity = 0.557), indicating unstable analytical framing.
• T-FC-L3 records high observed scores (7.63) with minimal YouTube reach, hinting at hidden adoption barriers.
• H-CR-L1 reports significant qualitative improvement despite a modest mean (6.35), potentially reflecting delayed entrainment not captured by the 1‑second survey.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted protocol refinements are emerging from convergent evidence.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10): investigate subgroup effects and consider recalibrating the Hz progression to reduce bimodality.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L3 (n=35): expand visibility and test a longer hold phase to capitalize on its strong efficacy and low YouTube exposure.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] H-OS-L1 (n=95): optimize Adviser routing to better match caregiver listener contexts and improve discovery.
• [SINGLE SOURCE] X-ME-L3 (n=18): adjust rhythm complexity to address mixed music opinions and improve consistency.
• [SINGLE SOURCE] H-CR-L3 (n=27): prototype an L2 variant with increased theta entrainment to probe the lower‑than‑expected mean.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding signals indicate evolving analytical interpretations.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: T-MB‑L1, T-MB‑L2, and especially T-MB‑L3 show similarity below 0.60, flagging substantial drift.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Maintenance‑Balance protocols (T‑MB‑L1/2/3) form a tight cluster, confirming cohesive design semantics.
• Confidence note: drift and clustering are based on three recent runs; while sufficient for trend spotting, longer histories would strengthen stability assessments.

Qualitative data reveal significant improvement for many and balanced rhythm perception. YouTube views are modest (8). AutoGluon predicts 7.54, modestly above observed. Distribution is near‑normal.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, confidence=developing) records the lowest mood efficacy (56% positive) with a bimodal pattern (noticeable vs no effect). Listeners settle quickly but music sentiment is mixed. YouTube reach is very low (4). AutoGluon forecasts 7.54, a sizable upward gap, reinforcing the need for rhythm or dosing adjustments. Distribution reflects subgroup divergence.
